---
title: "Week 5 Homework"
format:
  html: default
  pdf: default
---

::: {.callout-note title="Exercise"}
What is oeis.org? What information can you find about sequences there?
How might you use this resource for this course?
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Exercise"}
An 8-person committee is to be formed from a group of 15 women and 12
men. In how many ways can the committee be formed if the committee must
contain four men and four women? if there must be at least two men? if
there must be more women than men?
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Exercise"}
A **palindrome** is a string whose reverse is identical to the original
string. Give examples of bit strings of length 5 and 6 that are
palindromes. How many bit string palindromes of length $n$ are there?
How many ternary string palindromes of length $n$ are there?
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Exercise"}
How many strings of 6 letters of the english alphabet contain one vowel?
exactly two vowels? at least one vowel? at least two vowels? What is
number of strings of length $n$ with exactly $k$ vowels?
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Exercise"}
A string that contains only 0s,1s, and 2s is called a **ternary
string**. How many strings of 10 ternary digits (0, 1, or 2) are there
that contain exactly two 0s, three 1s, and five 2s? Generalize this to
the number of ternary strings of length $k$ with $a$ 0's, $b$ 1's, and
$c$ 2's where $a+b+c=k$.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Exercise"}
How many integer solutions are there to the equation $x+y+z=8$ for
which: $x,y ,z$ and are all positive?
$x,y ,z$ and are all non-negative?
$x,y ,z$ and are all greater than or equal to -3.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Exercise"}
How many ways are there to place 15 identical balls into 5 labeled
boxes:\
Each box has at least one ball?\
Each box has at least two balls?\
One box has exactly 10 balls?\
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Exercise"}
Suppose a professor has 10 identical calculators. How many ways are
there to give these calculators to 3 students such that:\
there are no other conditions?\
Each student gets 1 calculator?\
Each student gets 2 calculators?\
Each student gets 3 calculators?\
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Exercise"}
Your favorite math themed fast food drive-in offers 20 flavors which can
be added to your soda. You have enough money to buy a large soda with 4
added flavors. How many different soda concoctions can you order if:

You refuse to use any of the flavors more than once?\
You refuse repeats but care about the order the flavors are added?\
You allow yourself multiple shots of the same flavor?\
You allow yourself multiple shots, and care about the order the flavors
are added?\
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Proof"}
Prove $\binom{n}{k}\binom{n-k}{l}=\binom{n}{l}\binom{n-l}{k}$ with
$k+l \leq n$.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Proof"}
Let $m$ and $n$ be two integers. Prove that $mn+m$ is odd if and only if
$m$ is odd and $n$ is even. Hint: You will need two subproofs. one for
the if part and one for the only if part.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Proof"}
Prove that $n^2+n+1$ is odd for every integer $n$ by direct proof.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Proof"}
Let $n$ be a positive integer. Show that
$\binom{2n}{n+1}+\binom{2n}{n}=\frac{1}{2}\binom{2n+2}{n+1}$.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Proof"}
Prove
$$\frac{1}{1 \cdot 4}+\frac{1}{4 \cdot 7}+\frac{1}{7 \cdot 10}+ \cdots \frac{1}{(3n-2)(3n+1)}=\frac{n}{3n+1}$$
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Proof"}
Prove the hexagon property:
$$\binom{n-1}{k-1}\binom{n}{k+1}\binom{n+1}{k}=\binom{n-1}{k}\binom{n+1}{k+1}\binom{n}{k-1}$$
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Proof"}
$\bigstar$ Prove
$$\sum_{k\ge0}^{}\binom{r}{k}\binom{s}{n-k}=\binom{r+s}{n}$$ Hint: See
Example 1.10 on page 9 of the course text.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Proof"}
$\bigstar$ Prove
$$\sum_{k\ge0}^{}k\binom{m}{k}\binom{n}{k}=n\binom{m+n-1}{n}$$ Hint: See
Example 1.10 and 1.11 on page 9 of the course text.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Proof"}
$\bigstar$ Use induction to prove that
$1+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}+\ldots+\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\leq 2 \sqrt{n}$
for all positive integers $n$.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Proof"}
$\bigstar$ Using induction, prove that
$(\cos{x}+i\sin{x})^n=\cos{nx}+i\sin{nx}$ for $n\ge0$. Hint: Use the
angle addition formulas.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Proof"}
$\bigstar$ Prove

$$
\sum_{i=0}^{\lfloor n/2 \rfloor} \binom{n}{2i} = 2^{n-1}
$$

where $\lfloor x \rfloor$ is the floor function.
:::
